trade.summary: Arabia Saudita is a major crude oil supplier to Thailandia. Thailandia exports food products (especially chicken — previously a major category), automobiles, electronics, and manufacturing goods to Arabia Saudita. The trade relationship has a difficult history: Thai-Saudi relations were severed for decades following the "Blue Diamond Affair" (1989 jewel theft scandal involving Thai workers and alleged assassination of Saudi diplomats). Full diplomatic normalization only occurred in 2022, opening new trade potential.
disputes: Legacy of the Blue Diamond Affair (1989 jewel theft — relations frozen for 30+ years)

diplomatic.status: normalizing — full diplomatic relations restored 2022 after 30-year rupture
diplomatic.summary: Saudi-Thai relations are genuinely historic for their dramatic rupture and recent restoration. The 1989 Blue Diamond Affair — in which a Thai worker stole Saudi royal jewels, and subsequent murders of Saudi diplomats in Bangkok were allegedly linked to the case — caused Arabia Saudita to freeze diplomatic relations with Thailandia for over 30 years. Full ambassadorial relations were restored in January 2022. Both governments are actively working to build economic and political ties from a low base. The restoration opens significant trade and investment potential.
recent_incidents: Full diplomatic normalization January 2022 — historic rapprochement
Saudi Crown Prince MBS received Thai PM (2023)
Thailandia expressed commitment to resolve outstanding Blue Diamond Affair grievances

military.summary: No military alliance. Defence contacts are limited given the recent normalization. Some preliminary discussions on defence technology cooperation. Both countries are Stati Uniti security partners in their respective regions.

financial.summary: Financial ties are growing post-normalization. Arabia Saudita's PIF has expressed interest in Thai infrastructure and tourism investments. Thai banks are establishing presence in the Gulf. The normalization creates a framework for banking and investment flows that were previously constrained. Saudi Aramco is interested in partnerships with PTT (Thailandia's national oil company).

energy.summary: Arabia Saudita supplies crude oil to Thailandia's refineries, making it one of Thailandia's top energy suppliers. PTT and Saudi Aramco have cooperation potential in refining and petrochemicals. Arabia Saudita's Vision 2030 renewable energy goals and Thailandia's energy transition create opportunities for technology cooperation.

scientific.summary: Scientific collaboration is nascent given the recent normalization. Some medical and agricultural research exchanges are planned. KAStati UnitiT has begun engaging Thai universities. The post-normalization environment offers significant growth potential in academic exchanges.
